d7b_reasoning: The strategy employs a mean reversion framework targeting oversold conditions via RSI < 35, zscore < -1.5, and realized volatility filters, exiting when price crosses back above SMA or RSI recovers. However, the entry logic exhibits internal tension: it requires close < bb_upper_24_2 (typically true most of the time) rather than close < bb_lower_24_2 as the description suggests, undermining coherence between stated intent and implementation. The exit paths are sensible: mean reversion completion via SMA crossover or profit target. Theme alignment is strong—Bollinger bands, zscore, RSI, and SMA are all canonical mean reversion indicators from the hint vocabulary. Structural variant risk is very high: the factor set {bb_upper_24_2, close, realized_vol_24h, rsi_14, sma_24, zscore_48} matches prior hypothesis #21 exactly, and differs from #17 and #60 only by minor additions or removals. This appears to be a threshold parameter adjustment (RSI 35 vs other cutoffs, zscore -1.5 vs other values) rather than a structurally novel combination.
